prompt:
Hey! I know that studying in university can be difficult, but don't worry, it will get better with time managment. Here are some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time:
Make a scedule and stick to it - plan out your day including study time, breaks and relaxion time. Prioritize your tasks - focus on the most important ones first. Use a planner or a calendar to keep track of your assigments and deadlines. Try to be effecient with your study time, find a quiet place to concentrate. Take breaks between study session to refreash your mind. Stay physically active - exercise is a great way to reduse stress and improve focus. Make time for hobbies and activities you enjoy to relax. Remember to take care about yourself: eat well, get sleep enough and don't forget to have some fun!
Hang in there and your life will be easier!

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hey! I know that studying in university can be difficult, but don't worry, it will get better with time managment. Here are some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time:
Make a scedule and stick to it - plan out your day including study time, breaks and relaxion time. Prioritize your tasks - focus on the most important ones first. Use a planner or a calendar to keep track of your assigments and deadlines. Try to be effecient with your study time, find a quiet place to concentrate. Take breaks between study session to refreash your mind. Stay physically active - exercise is a great way to reduse stress and improve focus. Make time for hobbies and activities you enjoy to relax. Remember to take care about yourself: eat well, get sleep enough and don't forget to have some fun!
Hang in there and your life will be easier!
TEXT_TYPE: essay

CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: [{"ref_id":"sample_07010","cefr_level":"B1","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hey there! I've already finished the first semester, so I can help you. Here are some tips to have more free time:

	* Do tasks when they published, not at the last day. For example you thought that the homework will take one day, and after some time you realize that it is harder and you need to spend more time on it. But because you started earlier you have a lot of days before deadline, so you can do this homework easily.
	* Make plan and stick to it. You should know when you need to do certain homework, so you won't spend time on choosing task.
	* Don't forget to chill sometimes. You need to have at least one hour free time per day.
	* Sleep 7+ hours. If you think, that you can get more time by sleeping less -- you are wrong. You'll be exhausted the whole next day.

By following these tips I was able to pass all exams on score 8 or higher out of 10. Also, I had 1-2 hours free time each day and was able to participate in some olympiads/hackathons each weekends.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"they published,","labels":["Grammar"],"start":133,"end":148},{"text":"at the last day.","labels":["Grammar"],"start":153,"end":169},{"text":"For example you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":170,"end":185},{"text":"will","labels":["Grammar"],"start":212,"end":216},{"text":"realize","labels":["Grammar"],"start":255,"end":262},{"text":"is ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":271,"end":274},{"text":"need","labels":["Grammar"],"start":289,"end":293},{"text":"earlier you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":344,"end":355},{"text":"because","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":324,"end":331},{"text":"deadline","labels":["Grammar"],"start":382,"end":390},{"text":"plan","labels":["Grammar"],"start":436,"end":440},{"text":"homework, so","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":502,"end":514},{"text":"task","labels":["Grammar"],"start":548,"end":552},{"text":" think, that","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":669,"end":681},{"text":"-- you","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":721,"end":727},{"text":"tips I ","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":799,"end":806},{"text":"on score ","labels":["Grammar"],"start":833,"end":842},{"text":"hours free","labels":["Grammar"],"start":881,"end":891},{"text":"weekends","labels":["Grammar"],"start":968,"end":976}],"strengths":[{"text":"For example ","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":170,"end":182},{"text":"Also","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":865,"end":869},{"text":"You should know ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":458,"end":474},{"text":"stick to it","labels":["Lexical choice ✓"],"start":445,"end":456}],"comments":[["since"]]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07008","cefr_level":"B2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hey! I completely understand you and feel sorry you have to experience the lackage of free time. It's an awfull state, when your life seems not to belong to yourself. I faced  with the same problem during my first year in the university and here are some tips that helped me to organise my free time and to lead a harmonical study-work-life balance:

First of all, timetables really work. If you have a variety of things you should do, try to make an emphasis on the most significant ones and prioritise all task accoarding to the dependance on their importance and urgency. Then you should make a scedule for yourself, concidering all the details of your own limitations in time, mood, desire to study and moral state. For example, some people are more productive in the morning, whereas the others - in the evening, or even at night. Also this timetable will help you to remember all your deadlines, to be more prepared for all control works and to do everything in time.

Secondly, don't forget about your social life and communication! Choose a couple of hours in a week, in which you allow yourself just to have a rest. You can meet your friends, read a book, do some hobby - whatever you want, but it shouldn't be conncted with your university at all. During these hours you won't lose a lot of time, instead you get motivation, happiness and relax.

And the last advice - always remember, that your assesments don't determine your personality. In a couple of years you will hardly remember a bad mark for a control work, about which you worry now.
